verdict_reasoning: EREG encodes a 162-aa type I single-pass transmembrane proligand (pro-EREG, ~19 kDa glycosylated to ~55 kDa at the cell surface). It traffics to the plasma membrane as a bona fide surface form capable of juxtacrine signaling, but is constitutively and rapidly cleaved by ADAM17 (and ADAM9/12/15) within ~1–2 h to release the 46-aa (~6 kDa) soluble mature peptide — the canonical biological actor. The TM precursor stage is real and outer-leaflet-accessible, but short-lived and the shed soluble form dominates biological signaling. This matches the 'transient TM precursor of a shed-ligand-dominant gene' pattern → contextual/dual_localization.
